prerequisites: A Buildkite account with access to the organization whose CI/CD data you want to sync.
A Buildkite API access token (used for the connector's api_key field).
A start date (start_date), which sets the earliest point in time from which the connector reads data.

authMethods.name: API access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single secret field, api_key, which holds a Buildkite REST API access token. Buildkite authenticates REST API requests by setting the Authorization HTTP header to the word Bearer, followed by a space, followed by the access token. Tokens can be scoped to specific organizations and permissions and can be revoked at any time from the web interface.

setupSteps.title: Create a Buildkite API access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Buildkite and open Personal Settings > API Access Tokens, then choose New API Access Token. Select the Buildkite organization the token should access and set a token expiry. The new token is shown once on creation, so copy it immediately.

setupSteps.title: Grant the required REST API scopes
setupSteps.body: On the token form, select the REST API Scopes the connector needs to read your CI/CD data, for example read_organizations, read_pipelines, read_builds, and read_agents. Grant only read scopes; do not enable write scopes unless required. Buildkite also lets you optionally restrict the token to specific IP addresses via CIDR notation.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the Buildkite access token into the api_key field. Set start_date to the earliest date from which data should be synced. Both api_key and start_date are required.

setupSteps.title: Note rate limits
setupSteps.body: The Buildkite REST API enforces rate limiting and returns a 429 Too Many Requests response when a limit is exceeded. If syncs return rate-limit responses, reduce request frequency or sync scope. See the Buildkite REST API rate limiting documentation for current limits.

sources.title: Managing API access tokens (scopes and creation)
